provenance.source_corpus: Natural Recovery of Rough Fescue ( Festuca hallii (Vasey) Piper) Grassland After Disturbance by Pipeline Construction in Central Alberta, Canada

source_spans.text: Plow-in pipeline construction reduced disturbance intensity with retention of much of the original sod.
Pipeline construction techniques affected F. hallii cover, which was significantly greater on plow-in pipelines
wheatgrass cover, which was significantly greater on ditch-witch and topsoil-strip pipelines
Festuca hallii, moss and lichen, and litter cover were significantly greater on controls than ROWs
bare ground cover were significantly lower on controls than ROWs
Festuca hallii, moss and lichen, and litter cover were significantly greater on controls than ROWs
Festuca hallii, moss and lichen, and litter cover were significantly greater on controls than ROWs
Plow-in pipelines had the fewest non-native species
greatest rough fescue recovery
were similar to native grassland
Wheatgrass-dominated pipelines were associated with the greatest disturbance in our study
retention of much of the original sod
critical to successful recovery
recover better with plow-in pipeline construction than with seeding
importance of minimum disturbance in grasslands
Retaining grassland sod through plow-in pipeline construction

edges.subject: causal_mosaic:node:0e13936c9cfc
edges.original_sentence: Pipeline construction techniques affected F. hallii cover, which was significantly greater on plow-in pipelines, and wheatgrass cover, which was significantly greater on ditch-witch and topsoil-strip pipelines (Figure 3).

source_spans.text: Pipeline construction techniques affected F. hallii cover, which was significantly greater on plow-in pipelines, and wheatgrass cover, which was significantly greater on ditch-witch and topsoil-strip pipelines (Figure 3).

edges.original_sentence: Rough fescue appears to recover better with plow-in pipeline construction than with seeding, most likely from intact sod.
